things.name: Autostadt
things.description: Volkswagen's extraordinary 25-hectare automotive theme park next to the VW factory with 12 pavilions, glass car towers and immersive interactive exhibits

things.name: VW Factory Tour
things.description: Guided tours of the legendary Volkswagen Transparent Factory and main Wolfsburg production lines; see how Golf and Polo cars are built live

things.name: Phaeno Science Centre
things.description: Zaha Hadid-designed science museum with over 250 hands-on experiments in a dramatic concrete building; one of Europe's most innovative science centres

things.name: Kunstmuseum Wolfsburg
things.description: Leading contemporary art museum with major changing exhibitions and an outstanding collection of international modern and contemporary art

things.name: Wolfsburg Castle
things.description: 14th-century moated castle that gave the city its name, set in parkland on the Aller river north of the city centre with a charming restaurant

things.name: Allerpark
things.description: Large urban recreation park on the Aller river with a lake, beach, water sports, cycling paths and Wolfsburg's main outdoor leisure area

things.name: Stadt Gallery
things.description: Municipal art gallery presenting temporary exhibitions of modern and contemporary art in the heart of the purpose-built city centre

things.name: Autostadt Car Towers
things.description: The iconic twin glass cylindrical towers at Autostadt store 800 new Volkswagen vehicles each and have become Wolfsburg's most recognisable landmark

things.name: Wolfsburg City Library (Stadtbibliothek)
things.description: Award-winning modern library in the city centre hub, reflecting Wolfsburg's ambition as a model planned city of cultural and intellectual resources

things.name: Volkswagen Arena
things.description: Home of VfL Wolfsburg with a 30,000-seat capacity; the club won the 2009 Bundesliga and the 2015 DFB-Pokal and has competed in the Champions League
